articles.title: Bus hits cars in Virginia, killing 5 people and injuring 34, state police say
articles.description:
The five people killed were from Massachusetts.

The post Bus hits cars in Virginia, killing 5 people and injuring 34, state police say appeared first on Boston.com.
summary: Bus accident in Virginia resulted in 5 fatalities and 34 injuries.
All five deceased were residents of Massachusetts.
The incident raises concerns for Boston families traveling out of state.

articles.title: Mass. man reports beating Mount Everest speed climbing record
articles.description:
“The fitness is the easy part,” Tyler Andrews said in 2025. “The challenge is managing all the variables on the mountain itself.”

The post Mass. man reports beating Mount Everest speed climbing record appeared first on Boston.com.
summary: Tyler Andrews from Massachusetts claims to have set a new Mount Everest speed climbing record.
Andrews achieved this feat in 2025, showcasing his skills in extreme conditions.
His accomplishment inspires local climbers and highlights Massachusetts' growing presence in extreme sports.

articles.title: You may complain about the T, but a new ranking says it’s one of the best in the U.S.
articles.description:
The ranking emphasized Boston’s extensive transit options.

The post You may complain about the T, but a new ranking says it’s one of the best in the U.S. appeared first on Boston.com.
summary: Boston's transit system ranks among the best in the U.S. according to a new report.
The ranking highlights Boston's extensive transit options, benefiting over 1.5 million daily riders.
Improved perception of the T may encourage more residents to utilize public transportation.

articles.title: Wu gets rid of green infrastructure office, lays off director
articles.description:
The director's position was eliminated in the fiscal year 2027 budget, and the Office of Green Infrastructure will be incorporated into the Streets Cabinet.

The post Wu gets rid of green infrastructure office, lays off director appeared first on Boston.com.
summary: Mayor Wu eliminated the Office of Green Infrastructure in the FY 2027 budget.
The director's position was cut, affecting local green project oversight.
Green initiatives will now fall under the Streets Cabinet, impacting future infrastructure planning.

articles.title: Boston reports uptick in mpox cases
articles.description:
The city will be offering mpox vaccinations during the Pride flag-raising event at Boston City Hall on Monday, June 1.

The post Boston reports uptick in mpox cases appeared first on Boston.com.
summary: Boston reports an increase in mpox cases ahead of Pride Month.
Mpox vaccinations will be available on June 1 at City Hall.
Residents encouraged to get vaccinated to protect community health.
